english: gos (*gos*) a garment made of fine linen. — 2. v. śá-sna (*sha sna*), sub śa (*sha*).
a thick blanket *Ld.*

*Cs. W., C., B.* a hart, a stag, col. usually *\*śa-wa-ra-cù\** (*sha wa ra cu*) or *\*ru-ću\** (*ru cu*); *\*śá-p'o\** (*sha pho*) the male animal, *\*śá-mo\** (*sha mo*) the hind, roe, *\*śa-p'rúg\** (*sha phrug*) a young deer, fawn; cf. k'a-śwa (*kha shwa*).
1. after-birth, placenta. — 2. an ordinary coat made of cloth which has not been napped *W.*
parsley *Ld.*

*C., B.* (*W.* *\*móg-śa\** (*mog sha*)) mushroom; the various species of fungus receive their appellations from their colour (dkar-śá (*dkar sha*), nag-śa (*nag sha*), smug-śa (*smug sha*), ser-śa (*ser sha*)) or from the place where they grow (kluṅs-śa (*klungs sha*), ću-śa (*chu sha*), lud-śa (*lud sha*), śiṅ-śa (*shing sha*)); the damp climate of Sikkim produces moreover *\*ṣó-k'e\** (*sho khe*), kú-wa (*ku wa*) and ḍé-mo (*dre mo*) (sgre-mo (*sgre mo*)) *-śa-mo\** (*sha mo*), etc. *Cs.* has also śa-maṅ (*sha mang*), a thick kind of mushroom.
(cf. gśér-pa (*gsher pa*)) *W.* moist.
*Ssk.* n. of a bird, *Gracula religiosa*; a species of jay.
n. of one of the two principal disciples of Buddha.
1. hartshorn *Med.* — 2. n. of a vein *Med.*
warped, oblique, aslant *W.*
*Ssk.*: spear, lance, pike, sword, *Cs.* also trident; *Dzl.*
in śag-ter-gás (*shag ter gas*) it broke, it burst asunder *Sch.*

1. *C.* small stones or pebbles, gravel, śág-ma-ćaṅ (*shag ma can*) gravelly, śag-táṅ (*shag thang*) a plain abounding with gravel. — 2. *W.* pebble, śag-rúd (*shag rud*) rocky ground, covered with a thin layer of mould which only by dint of much irrigation will yield a scanty produce; śag-rúg (*shag rug*) gravel, śág-sa (*shag sa*) earth mixed up with pebbles, stony, sterile ground.

1. joke, jest, fun, śags ćé-ba byéd-pa (*shags che ba byed pa*) to rally maliciously, to turn into ridicule with sarcasms *Glr.*; ṅan-śágs (*ngan shags*) *Mil.* a bad joke; k'a-śágs (*kha shags*) v. k'a (*kha*). — 2. cause of a contention, object of a dispute or a quarrel, matter in dispute *Mil.*; quarrel, dispute, contention, in gen., *\*śag gyág-pa\** (*shag gyag pa*) *C.* to fight, to quarrel, to dispute.
v. gśaṅ (*gshang*).

wylie: shang po, shang spyang can, shang rig
english: *\*śaṅ kúl-će\** (*shang kul ce*), śaṅ-rig (*shang rig*) v. gćaṅ-po (*gtsang po*); *\*śaṅ-lág\** (*shang lag*) a kind of fur, perh. for spyaṅ-slág (*spyang slag*) fur-coat of wolf's skin *Lh.*
sabre, sword *Pth.*
a fabulous creature with wings and bird's feet, but otherwise like a human being; śaṅ-śaṅ-téu (*shang shang te'u*) *Cs*: pheasant or partridge
resp. for sna (*sna*), the nose, śaṅs-rgyúd (*shangs rgyud*) *Pth.*, śaṅs-sna (*shangs sna*) id.; śaṅs-rtse (*shangs rtse*) tip of the nose.

1. the mark of punctuation: །, also rkyaṅ-śád (*rkyang shad*) or ćig-śád (*cig shad*); it is a diacritical sign of about the value of our comma or semicolon; ñis-śád (*nyis shad*) the double shad, ༎, dividing sentences, or, in metrical compositions, verses; bźi-śád (*bzhi shad*) the fourfold shad, །།།།, at the end of sections and chapters; tseg-śád (*tseg shad*) the dotted shad (༑), an ornamental form of the ordinary shad, always made use of, when a shad is to be put after the first syllable of a line; śad byéd-pa (*shad byed pa*) *Lex.*, ṭéṅ-pa (*theng pa*) *Sch.*, to make a shad. — 2. v. the following article.

wylie: shad pa, gshad pa, bshad pa
english: *Cs.* 1. to comb, to curry, (a horse), also śad rgyág-pa (*shad rgyag pa*). — 2. to brush, to stroke, to rub gently with the hand *W.*; śád-ma (*shad ma*) *Sch.* curry-comb, horse-comb; *\*śiṅ-śe\** (*shing she*) a wooden rake, *\*ćág-śe\** (*lcags she*) an iron rake *C.*

1. iron hoop of a barrel *Cs.* — 2. small boat, *\*śém-pa\** (*shan pa*) ferry-man *C.* — 3. snow-leopard *W.* (cf. gsa (*gsa'*)). — 4. difference, distinction, śan byéd-pa (*shan byed pa*) to distinguish, decide, determine *Mil.* and elsewh, gźan-gyis śan mi byéd-pas (*gzhan gyis shan mi byed pas*) as nobody else is able to decide it *Glr.*; skad-gñis-śan-sbyór (*skad gnyis shan sbyor*) is said to be the title of a certain dictionary.
1. oblique *W.*, *\*śàṅ-k'a-la ḍé-će\** (*shan kha la dre ce*) to cut off obliquely; śàṅ-tér (*shan ter*) id.,
